devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । तस्यां स पाञ्चजन्यां वै विष्णुमायोपबृंहितः । हर्यश्वसंज्ञानयुतं पुत्रानजनयद्विभुः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācatasyāṃ sa pāñcajanyāṃ vai viṣṇumāyopabṛṃhitaḥharyaśvasaṃjñānayutaṃ putrānajanayadvibhuḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Being imbued with the energy of Viṣṇu, Dakṣa became powerful and begot through that (above-mentioned) Pāñcajanī ten thousand sons called Haryaśva.

devanagari: अपृथग्धर्मशीलास्ते सर्वे दाक्षायणा नृप । पित्रा प्रोक्ताः प्रजासर्गे प्रतीचीं प्रययुर्दिशम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: apṛthagdharmaśīlāste sarve dākṣāyaṇā nṛpapitrā proktāḥ prajāsarge pratīcīṃ prayayurdiśam
translation: Oh King! All those sons of Dakṣa were of the same nature and similarly righteous in conduct. When they were commanded by the father (Dakṣa) to beget children, they went to the western direction.

devanagari: तत्र नारायणसरस्तीर्थं सिन्धुसमुद्रयोः । सङ्गमो यत्र सुमहन्मुनिसिद्धनिषेवितम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra nārāyaṇasarastīrthaṃ sindhusamudrayoḥsaṅgamo yatra sumahanmunisiddhaniṣevitam
translation: They went to a sacred lake called Nārāyaṇasaras[1] situated in the area where the river Sindhu meets the sea—a holy place resorted to by a number of sages and Siddhas.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
A lake at the mouth of the Indus at the western extremity of the Runn of Kacch, 18 miles south-west of Lakhpat—GDAMI 138.

devanagari: तदुपस्पर्शनादेव विनिर्धूतमलाशयाः । धर्मे पारमहंस्ये च प्रोत्पन्नमतयोऽप्युत ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tadupasparśanādeva vinirdhūtamalāśayāḥdharme pāramahaṃsye ca protpannamatayo'pyuta
translation: At the very touch of that (sacred water of the) lake, their hearts were cleansed of all impurities and they found their mind attracted to the righteous way of life of the Paramahaṃsas (the highest order of ascetics).

devanagari: तेपिरे तप एवोग्रं पित्रादेशेन यन्त्रिताः । प्रजाविवृद्धये यत्तान्देवर्षिस्तान्ददर्श ह ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tepire tapa evograṃ pitrādeśena yantritāḥprajāvivṛddhaye yattāndevarṣistāndadarśa ha
translation: Being restrained by the command of their father for begetting prolific progeny, they practised severe austerities. It is then, they say, that the celestial sage Nārada saw them.

devanagari: उवाच चाथ हर्यश्वाः कथं स्रक्ष्यथ वै प्रजाः । अदृष्ट्वान्तं भुवो यूयं बालिशा बत पालकाः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: uvāca cātha haryaśvāḥ kathaṃ srakṣyatha vai prajāḥadṛṣṭvāntaṃ bhuvo yūyaṃ bāliśā bata pālakāḥ
translation:
And he (Nārada) spoke unto them:
‘Oh Haryaśvas! How is it that without seeing the limits of the earth you want to create children. You are certainly childish (foolish). It is a pity that protectors (of the subjects) as you are, you are really childish.

devanagari: तथैकपुरुषं राष्ट्रं बिलं चादृष्टनिर्गमम् । बहुरूपां स्त्रियं चापि पुमांसं पुंश्चलीपतिम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tathaikapuruṣaṃ rāṣṭraṃ bilaṃ cādṛṣṭanirgamambahurūpāṃ striyaṃ cāpi pumāṃsaṃ puṃścalīpatim
translation: Without seeing a kingdom inhabited by only one man, and without knowing the hole with (apparently) invisible outlet, the woman who can assume different forms (at will), and the man who espouses a harlot, a river flowing in both directions, a wonderful house constructed of twenty-five materials, at one place a swan telling miraculous tales, an independent, revolving (strong, sharp) disc made of adamants and razors, and without properly understanding the implication of the command of your omniscient father, how will you proceed with your job of creation?

devanagari: नदीमुभयतो वाहां पञ्चपञ्चाद्भुतं गृहम् । क्वचिद्धंसं चित्रकथं क्षौरपव्यं स्वयं भ्रमिम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: nadīmubhayato vāhāṃ pañcapañcādbhutaṃ gṛhamkvaciddhaṃsaṃ citrakathaṃ kṣaurapavyaṃ svayaṃ bhramim
translation: Without seeing a kingdom inhabited by only one man, and without knowing the hole with (apparently) invisible outlet, the woman who can assume different forms (at will), and the man who espouses a harlot, a river flowing in both directions, a wonderful house constructed of twenty-five materials, at one place a swan telling miraculous tales, an independent, revolving (strong, sharp) disc made of adamants and razors, and without properly understanding the implication of the command of your omniscient father, how will you proceed with your job of creation?

devanagari: कथं स्वपितुरादेशमविद्वांसो विपश्चितः । अनुरूपमविज्ञाय अहो सर्गं करिष्यथ ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: kathaṃ svapiturādeśamavidvāṃso vipaścitaḥanurūpamavijñāya aho sargaṃ kariṣyatha
translation: Without seeing a kingdom inhabited by only one man, and without knowing the hole with (apparently) invisible outlet, the woman who can assume different forms (at will), and the man who espouses a harlot, a river flowing in both directions, a wonderful house constructed of twenty-five materials, at one place a swan telling miraculous tales, an independent, revolving (strong, sharp) disc made of adamants and razors, and without properly understanding the implication of the command of your omniscient father, how will you proceed with your job of creation?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । तन्निशम्याथ हर्यश्वा औत्पत्तिकमनीषया । वाचः कूटं तु देवर्षेः स्वयं विममृशुर्धिया ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācatanniśamyātha haryaśvā autpattikamanīṣayāvācaḥ kūṭaṃ tu devarṣeḥ svayaṃ vimamṛśurdhiyā
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having heard the enigmatic words of the divine sage (Nārada) the Haryaśvas, with their own inborn reasoning capacity, began to investigate with their intelligence.

devanagari: भूः क्षेत्रं जीवसंज्ञं यदनादि निजबन्धनम् । अदृष्ट्वा तस्य निर्वाणं किमसत्कर्मभिर्भवेत् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūḥ kṣetraṃ jīvasaṃjñaṃ yadanādi nijabandhanamadṛṣṭvā tasya nirvāṇaṃ kimasatkarmabhirbhavet
translation: The earth (bhū) is that field (or the liṅga-śarīra—the subtle body—which causes pleasure, pain, merit, demerit etc. like an agricultural farm yielding crop of cereals) designated as jīva. The subtle body is (practically) beginningless (existing from times immemorial) and it acts as a bondage to the Soul. Without seeing how it becomes extinct, what is the use of impious karmas (which do not lead to Mokṣa).

devanagari: एक एवेश्वरस्तुर्यो भगवान्स्वाश्रयः परः । तमदृष्ट्वाभवं पुंसः किमसत्कर्मभिर्भवेत् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: eka eveśvarasturyo bhagavānsvāśrayaḥ paraḥtamadṛṣṭvābhavaṃ puṃsaḥ kimasatkarmabhirbhavet
translation: The only one (man in the country) stands for the Sole Almighty ruler (of the universe). He is (beyond and) witness to all mental states, viz., viśva, taijasa and prājña) and hence is called the fourth—the omnipresent. He is self-dependent supreme (the cause of everything else). Without seeing that birthless (eternally free) Lord, what purpose can be served by acts not dedicated to him?

devanagari: पुमान्नैवैति यद्गत्वा बिलस्वर्गं गतो यथा । प्रत्यग्धामाविद इह किमसत्कर्मभिर्भवेत् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: pumānnaivaiti yadgatvā bilasvargaṃ gato yathāpratyagdhāmāvida iha kimasatkarmabhirbhavet
translation: Just as a person who has gone to the subterranean heaven (pātāla) does not return (to the earth in the same body), a man does not return to saṃsāra after attaining to the self-illuminating Brahman within him. To a man who has not realized (lit. is ignorant of) the Inner Light—Self-effulgent Brahman, worthless karmas (which may lead to heavenly happiness and other transitory pleasures) are of no avail.

devanagari: नानारूपात्मनो बुद्धिः स्वैरिणीव गुणान्विता । तन्निष्ठामगतस्येह किमसत्कर्मभिर्भवेत् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: nānārūpātmano buddhiḥ svairiṇīva guṇānvitātanniṣṭhāmagatasyeha kimasatkarmabhirbhavet
translation: Like a loose wanton woman wearing various attractive forms and gifted with (many attractive) qualities, the intellect of man assumes shapes of objects of lower type of pleasures (like form, taste and other objects of senses) and is influenced by guṇas (like rajas, tamas). To a person who has not put an end to (i.e. transcended) such a multiple-interested intellect, what can be achieved through hot pursuit of such activities?

devanagari: तत्सङ्गभ्रंशितैश्वर्यं संसरन्तं कुभार्यवत् । तद्गतीरबुधस्येह किमसत्कर्मभिर्भवेत् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: tatsaṅgabhraṃśitaiśvaryaṃ saṃsarantaṃ kubhāryavattadgatīrabudhasyeha kimasatkarmabhirbhavet
translation: What can be achieved through acts done without discernment and knowledge by a jīva (man) who does not understand the loss of his independence (and non-attachment) by his association with such (above-mentioned) intellect and is like a husband of a loose wife, follows her ways (characterised by pleasure and pain).

devanagari: सृष्ट्यप्ययकरीं मायां वेलाकूलान्तवेगिताम् । मत्तस्य तामविज्ञस्य किमसत्कर्मभिर्भवेत् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sṛṣṭyapyayakarīṃ māyāṃ velākūlāntavegitāmmattasya tāmavijñasya kimasatkarmabhirbhavet
translation: What can be gained from activities influenced by Māyā which causes both the creation and destruction (and is like a river flowing both ways). (In order to prevent people fallen in it from reaching the banks) it rushes forth with great velocity (assuming the form of anger, egotism etc.) at the ghats, and its banks (in the form of penance, learning etc.).

devanagari: पञ्चविंशतितत्त्वानां पुरुषोऽद्भुतदर्पणः । अध्यात्ममबुधस्येह किमसत्कर्मभिर्भवेत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: pañcaviṃśatitattvānāṃ puruṣo'dbhutadarpaṇaḥadhyātmamabudhasyeha kimasatkarmabhirbhavet
translation: Puruṣa (the Inner Controller abiding in the body) is a wonderful basis and mirror[2] reflecting the twenty-five categories (enumerated by Sāṃkhyas). Of what use are the acts (done with a false sense of independence) by one who has not realized that he (Puruṣa) presides over the body (the aggregate of the categories—a collection of cause and effect).[3]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā reads adbhuta-darśana ‘One who is of wonderful form’ (āscarya-rūpa).

Footnote [3]:
Sāṅkhyas differ According to Īśvarakṛṣṇa, Prakṛti is uncreated (without any cause). The next seven categories from mahat to tanmātras are both cause (of the next category) and the effect (of the previous category) and the next sixteen: mind, 5 gross elements, 10 sense-organs are effects only. Puruṣa is neither the cause nor effect.—Sāṅkhyakārikā 3.

devanagari: ऐश्वरं शास्त्रमुत्सृज्य बन्धमोक्षानुदर्शनम् । विविक्तपदमज्ञाय किमसत्कर्मभिर्भवेत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: aiśvaraṃ śāstramutsṛjya bandhamokṣānudarśanamviviktapadamajñāya kimasatkarmabhirbhavet
translation: (This explains kvacid haṃsaṃ citrakatham in verse 8 above). What purpose will be served by acts oriented to the external phenomenal world if one neglects (the study of) the śāstra (philosophic teaching) which establishes the existence of the Almighty Ruler by distinguishing between the intelligent principle (the Soul) and inert matter and shows the (nature of) bondage and (Final) Liberation (like a swan drinking milk only from the mixture of milk and water)?

devanagari: कालचक्रं भ्रमिस्तीक्ष्णं सर्वं निष्कर्षयज्जगत् । स्वतन्त्रमबुधस्येह किमसत्कर्मभिर्भवेत् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kālacakraṃ bhramistīkṣṇaṃ sarvaṃ niṣkarṣayajjagatsvatantramabudhasyeha kimasatkarmabhirbhavet
translation:
(The explanation of kṣaurapavyaṃ svayam bhramim—verse 8 above).
The wheel of Time which is relentlessly and eternally revolving is sharp like razor-blade. It is absolutely independent (and hence uncontrollable). It destroys the whole creation. To him who does not know this (nature of Time), of what use are the worthless acts (done under the false presumption that their fruits are eternal).

devanagari: शास्त्रस्य पितुरादेशं यो न वेद निवर्तकम् । कथं तदनुरूपाय गुणविस्रम्भ्युपक्रमेत् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śāstrasya piturādeśaṃ yo na veda nivartakamkathaṃ tadanurūpāya guṇavisrambhyupakramet
translation:
(The inner significance of verse 9 above).
How can anyone who does not understand the precept of desisting from worldly activities, given by his father, the śāstra (which gives him the second birth by the upanayana, investiture of the sacred-thread-ceremony and tenders salutary advice) and who entertains faith in the path of worldly activities (Pravṛtti-mārga) based on guṇas, go ahead with the work of creation? (As Dakṣa ceases to be a father after upanayana, you can disregard his order without incurring a sin if you follow the precept of śāstra, your father, and follow the path of renunciation—Nivṛtti-dharma).

devanagari: इति व्यवसिता राजन्हर्यश्वा एकचेतसः । प्रययुस्तं परिक्रम्य पन्थानमनिवर्तनम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti vyavasitā rājanharyaśvā ekacetasaḥprayayustaṃ parikramya panthānamanivartanam
translation: (Being convinced by Nārada) Haryaśvas unanimously resolved thus, and circumambulating him (Nārada, by the right side as a mark of respect) went the way (to liberation) from which there is no return, Oh King!

devanagari: स्वरब्रह्मणि निर्भात हृषीकेशपदाम्बुजे । अखण्डं चित्तमावेश्य लोकाननुचरन्मुनिः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: svarabrahmaṇi nirbhāta hṛṣīkeśapadāmbujeakhaṇḍaṃ cittamāveśya lokānanucaranmuniḥ
translation: The sage Nārada, who has concentrated (lit. entered) his undivided mind in the lotus-like feet of Lord Viṣṇu (Hṛṣīkeśa—the Master of sense-organs) as revealed in Svara- Brahman (Brahman in the form of notes of the musical scale on his lute), went about (his own way in) the world.

devanagari: नाशं निशम्य पुत्राणां नारदाच्छीलशालिनाम् । अन्वतप्यत कः शोचन्सुप्रजस्त्वं शुचां पदम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: nāśaṃ niśamya putrāṇāṃ nāradācchīlaśālināmanvatapyata kaḥ śocansuprajastvaṃ śucāṃ padam
translation: Having heard that his sons, so excellent due to their righteous character, have disappeared and are lost to their original duty (of procreating children) due to the advice of Nārada, Dakṣa was tormented with sorrow and lamented that even to have good children can be a source of grief.

devanagari: स भूयः पाञ्चजन्यायामजेन परिसान्त्वितः । पुत्रानजनयद्दक्षः सवलाश्वान्सहस्रिणः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa bhūyaḥ pāñcajanyāyāmajena parisāntvitaḥputrānajanayaddakṣaḥ savalāśvānsahasriṇaḥ
translation: Being much consoled by god Brahmā, Dakṣa again procreated by Pañcajanī a thousand sons called Śabalāśva.

devanagari: ते च पित्रा समादिष्टाः प्रजासर्गे धृतव्रताः । नारायणसरो जग्मुर्यत्र सिद्धाः स्वपूर्वजाः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: te ca pitrā samādiṣṭāḥ prajāsarge dhṛtavratāḥnārāyaṇasaro jagmuryatra siddhāḥ svapūrvajāḥ
translation: Even they (Śabalāśvas) who were commanded by the father to beget progeny, took to religious vows and went to the (sacred) lake Nārāyaṇa where their elder brothers attained perfection (i.e. god-realization).

devanagari: तदुपस्पर्शनादेव विनिर्धूतमलाशयाः । जपन्तो ब्रह्म परमं तेपुस्तत्र महत्तपः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tadupasparśanādeva vinirdhūtamalāśayāḥjapanto brahma paramaṃ tepustatra mahattapaḥ
translation: With their minds and hearts perfectly cleansed of all impurities (sins) at the touch of those sacred waters, they practised austere penance muttering (all the while) the sacred syllable OM (a symbol of the Supreme Brahman).

devanagari: अब्भक्षाः कतिचिन्मासान्कतिचिद्वायुभोजनाः । आराधयन्मन्त्रमिममभ्यस्यन्त इडस्पतिम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: abbhakṣāḥ katicinmāsānkaticidvāyubhojanāḥārādhayanmantramimamabhyasyanta iḍaspatim
translation: For several months, they subsisted on water, and for many months, on the air only. They propitiated the Lord of all Mantras (the glorious god Viṣṇu), repeating-this (following) sacred mantra:

devanagari: ॐ नमो नारायणाय पुरुषाय महात्मने । विशुद्धसत्त्वधिष्ण्याय महाहंसाय धीमहि ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: oṃ namo nārāyaṇāya puruṣāya mahātmaneviśuddhasattvadhiṣṇyāya mahāhaṃsāya dhīmahi
translation: “OM. Salutations to Lord Nārāyaṇa, the Indwelling Controller, the Supreme Soul, whose place of manifestation is the extremely pure sāttvic heart and who is absolutely free from all blemishes. We meditate upon him.”

devanagari: इति तानपि राजेन्द्र प्रजासर्गधियो मुनिः । उपेत्य नारदः प्राह वाचः कूटानि पूर्ववत् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti tānapi rājendra prajāsargadhiyo muniḥupetya nāradaḥ prāha vācaḥ kūṭāni pūrvavat
translation: The sage Nārada approached even those (sons of Dakṣa) who made up their minds to renew creation and spoke to them the enigmatic words similar to those addressed to their (elder) brothers, Oh King of Kings.

devanagari: दाक्षायणाः संशृणुत गदतो निगमं मम । अन्विच्छतानुपदवीं भ्रातॄणां भ्रातृवत्सलाः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: dākṣāyaṇāḥ saṃśṛṇuta gadato nigamaṃ mamaanvicchatānupadavīṃ bhrātṝṇāṃ bhrātṛvatsalāḥ
translation: “Oh sons of Dakṣa! Listen carefully to the precept as given (lit. addressed) by me. You find out the path followed by your elder brothers (for whom you are so full of affection).

devanagari: भ्रातॄणां प्रायणं भ्राता योऽनुतिष्ठति धर्मवित् । स पुण्यबन्धुः पुरुषो मरुद्भिः सह मोदते ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: bhrātṝṇāṃ prāyaṇaṃ bhrātā yo'nutiṣṭhati dharmavitsa puṇyabandhuḥ puruṣo marudbhiḥ saha modate
translation: A brother who knows the path of righteousness, follows the great path trodden by his brothers and with merit as his friend, he enjoys (celestial) life in the company of the Marut—(wind) gods (noted for their fraternal affection).”

devanagari: एतावदुक्त्वा प्रययौ नारदोऽमोघदर्शनः । तेऽपि चान्वगमन्मार्गं भ्रातॄणामेव मारिष ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvaduktvā prayayau nārado'moghadarśanaḥte'pi cānvagamanmārgaṃ bhrātṝṇāmeva māriṣa
translation: Oh noble King! having addressed this much, Nārada whose audience is never unfruitful, departed. They (Śabalāśvas) also followed the (spiritual) path of their (elder) brothers.

devanagari: सध्रीचीनं प्रतीचीनं परस्यानुपथं गताः । नाद्यापि ते निवर्तन्ते पश्चिमा यामिनीरिव ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: sadhrīcīnaṃ pratīcīnaṃ parasyānupathaṃ gatāḥnādyāpi te nivartante paścimā yāminīriva
translation: Having resorted to the exalted and agreeable path attainable to those whose senses and minds are turned within (away from the external world)—the path that leads to the realization of the Supreme Brahman—they, like the nights that have passed, never return to this day.

devanagari: एतस्मिन्काल उत्पातान्बहून्पश्यन्प्रजापतिः । पूर्ववन्नारदकृतं पुत्रनाशमुपाशृणोत् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: etasminkāla utpātānbahūnpaśyanprajāpatiḥpūrvavannāradakṛtaṃ putranāśamupāśṛṇot
translation: Noticing at this time, a number of evil portents, the Prājāpati Dakṣa came to know that Nārada had ruined his sons.

devanagari: चुक्रोध नारदायासौ पुत्रशोकविमूर्च्छितः । देवर्षिमुपलभ्याह रोषाद्विस्फुरिताधरः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: cukrodha nāradāyāsau putraśokavimūrcchitaḥdevarṣimupalabhyāha roṣādvisphuritādharaḥ
translation: Overwhelmed with grief for his sons, he got enraged with Nārada. (To prevent Dakṣa from turning into a recluse,. Nārada approached him). Finding Nārada, he exploded (lit. spoke to him) with his lower lip quivering with rage.

devanagari: श्रीदक्ष उवाच । अहो असाधो साधूनां साधुलिङ्गेन नस्त्वया । असाध्वकार्यर्भकाणां भिक्षोर्मार्गः प्रदर्शितः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīdakṣa uvācaaho asādho sādhūnāṃ sādhuliṅgena nastvayāasādhvakāryarbhakāṇāṃ bhikṣormārgaḥ pradarśitaḥ
translation:
Dakṣa said:
Oh wicked fellow donning the garb of holy man! You have done a great harm to good people like us, in showing the path of mendicants to my young (good-natured) children.

devanagari: ऋणैस्त्रिभिरमुक्तानाममीमांसितकर्मणाम् । विघातः श्रेयसः पाप लोकयोरुभयोः कृतः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṇaistribhiramuktānāmamīmāṃsitakarmaṇāmvighātaḥ śreyasaḥ pāpa lokayorubhayoḥ kṛtaḥ
translation: Oh sinful person! You have deprived (my sons) of the bliss in both the worlds (here and hereafter),as they have not repaid their debts to the sages, gods and manes and have not investigated the nature of karmas.

devanagari: एवं त्वं निरनुक्रोशो बालानां मतिभिद्धरेः । पार्षदमध्ये चरसि यशोहा निरपत्रपः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ tvaṃ niranukrośo bālānāṃ matibhiddhareḥpārṣadamadhye carasi yaśohā nirapatrapaḥ
translation: Merciless and prejudicer of the mind of children as you are, you are bringing stigma to the glory of Lord Hari, and shameless as you are, you still move among the attendants of Hari.

devanagari: ननु भागवता नित्यं भूतानुग्रहकातराः । ऋते त्वां सौहृदघ्नं वै वैरङ्करमवैरिणाम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: nanu bhāgavatā nityaṃ bhūtānugrahakātarāḥṛte tvāṃ sauhṛdaghnaṃ vai vairaṅkaramavairiṇām
translation: With the exception of you who are verily a traitor to friends and who sow enmity between friends, all the devotees of Hari are? really ever eager to shower grace on living beings.

devanagari: नेत्थं पुंसां विरागः स्यात्त्वया केवलिना मृषा । मन्यसे यद्युपशमं स्नेहपाशनिकृन्तनम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: netthaṃ puṃsāṃ virāgaḥ syāttvayā kevalinā mṛṣāmanyase yadyupaśamaṃ snehapāśanikṛntanam
translation: Renunciation cannot in this way be generated in the minds of men by you who have disguised yourself as an Avaḍhūta even though you have no knowledge. Though you regard that self-control (automatically) severs the bonds of affection (it is wrong).

devanagari: नानुभूय न जानाति पुमान्विषयतीक्ष्णताम् । निर्विद्यते स्वयं तस्मान्न तथा भिन्नधीः परैः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: nānubhūya na jānāti pumānviṣayatīkṣṇatāmnirvidyate svayaṃ tasmānna tathā bhinnadhīḥ paraiḥ
translation: A man cannot realize how objects of sensual pleasures cause painful consequences, without personally experiencing them. Therefore, one does not get disgusted to that extent if his mind is unsettled by others (in that matter), as a person would who has undergone the experience himself.

devanagari: यन्नस्त्वं कर्मसन्धानां साधूनां गृहमेधिनाम् । कृतवानसि दुर्मर्षं विप्रियं तव मर्षितम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yannastvaṃ karmasandhānāṃ sādhūnāṃ gṛhamedhināmkṛtavānasi durmarṣaṃ vipriyaṃ tava marṣitam
translation: We have tolerated the unbearable wrong you have done to us, householders, who have vowed to perform Vedic karmas with a wish to get heavenly bliss.

devanagari: तन्तुकृन्तन यन्नस्त्वमभद्रमचरः पुनः । तस्माल्लोकेषु ते मूढ न भवेद्भ्रमतः पदम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tantukṛntana yannastvamabhadramacaraḥ punaḥtasmāllokeṣu te mūḍha na bhavedbhramataḥ padam
translation: You are a destroyer of the continuity of my race. As you have again done a wrong to us, you will not get a resting place while wandering through the worlds (Oh fool).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । प्रतिजग्राह तद्बाढं नारदः साधुसम्मतः । एतावान्साधुवादो हि तितिक्षेतेश्वरः स्वयम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācapratijagrāha tadbāḍhaṃ nāradaḥ sādhusammataḥetāvānsādhuvādo hi titikṣeteśvaraḥ svayam
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Nārada who was highly respected by the pious people accepted the curse saying, ‘Very well’. He is called really a Sādhu (pious soul) who though competent to retaliate, bears up the wrong done to him.
[back to top]
